3.why: If no pair is profitable, the answer is 0 (we don't trade).
3.invariant: max_profit holds the best profit found so far.

4.happening: Record how many days there are.
4.why: Both loops need the day count to know where to stop.
4.invariant: n is the number of days.

5.why: We will try selling on every day that comes after it.
5.invariant: i is the buy day for this round of trials.

6.happening: Pick a later day j to sell on.
6.why: Selling must happen after buying, so j starts at i+1.
6.invariant: j is a sell day strictly after i.

7.happening: Buy at prices[i] ({prices[i]}), sell at prices[j] ({prices[j]}) → profit {prices[j] - prices[i]}.
7.why: This is the gain of this one specific buy/sell pair.

8.happening: Is this pair's profit ({prices[j] - prices[i]}) better than the best ({max_profit})?
8.why: We keep only the largest profit across all pairs.

9.happening: New record — this pair is the best transaction so far.
9.why: It beats every pair tried before it.
9.invariant: max_profit is the largest profit among pairs tried.

10.happening: Every pair checked — return the best profit ({max_profit}).
10.why: The exhaustive search guarantees the true maximum, but at O(n²) cost.

init.happening: Preparing: zero profit and the day count.
init.why: We set up before pairing days off.

loop.happening: Choosing a buy day to hold fixed.
loop.why: Each buy day is paired with all later sell days.
loop.invariant: i is the current buy day.

move.why: Only days after the buy day are valid sells.
move.invariant: j runs from i+1 to the end.

check.happening: Comparing this pair's profit to the best.
check.why: We want the single most profitable pair.

update.why: This pair beats every earlier one.
